issues.title: Failed to send message to session xxxxxxx: Cannot invoke "org.apache.catalina.connector.OutputBuffer.isBlocking()" because "this.ob" is null

comments.body:
I'm getting same error with server dependencies as 

```
org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-dependencies:3.2.6

implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-mcp-server-webmvc-spring-boot-starter:1.0.0-M6'

```

and client is 

```
implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-mcp-client-spring-boot-starter:1.0.0-M6'
implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-anthropic-spring-boot-starter:1.0.0-M6'
```

The client almost always timeout after calling mcp server.

server log are also

-2025-05-12 22:22:09.230 -ERROR 68558 --- [oundedElastic-5] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         user= : Failed to send message to session xxxxxx: Cannot invoke "org.apache.catalina.connector.OutputBuffer.isBlocking()" because "this.ob" is null

Maybe spring boot latest version is not up to date with the fix in java sdk?

I also tried to implement with webflux, but the spring autoconfiguration call the tool in a sync manner and it break.

```
	@Override
	public String call(String functionInput) {
		Map<String, Object> arguments = ModelOptionsUtils.jsonToMap(functionInput);
		return this.asyncMcpClient.callTool(new CallToolRequest(this.getToolDefinition().name(), arguments))
			.map(response -> ModelOptionsUtils.toJsonString(response.content()))
			.block();
	}
```
.block() from AsyncMcpToolCallback

Is the problem solved？I'm also having the same issue.
2025-05-18T10:01:42.150+08:00 ERROR 10716 --- [image-search-mcp] [oundedElastic-1] i.m.s.t.WebMvcSseServerTransport         : Failed to send message to session ce1254a0-4bb7-4418-bd62-4c55fab84fa6: Cannot invoke "org.apache.catalina.connector.OutputBuffer.isBlocking()" because "this.ob" is null

Error Message:
"this.ob" is null
Failed to send message to session 107c61b0-e19a-4560-89c5-2fe1f29384d4: Cannot invoke "org.apache.catalina.connector.OutputBuffer.isBlocking()" because "this.ob" is null

Reason:
The client connection may have been disconnected or timed out during the call to the API.

Solution:
Add Tomcat timeout configuration to extend the connection timeout period:
yaml
server:
  tomcat:
    connection-timeout: 180000  # Set Tomcat connection timeout to 180,000 milliseconds (3 minutes)

Note:
The configured timeout value (180,000 ms) is within an acceptable range for most SSE (Server-Sent Events) scenarios, balancing between preventing premature disconnects and avoiding resource leaks from idle connections. Adjust this value based on your application's specific requirements for real-time data streaming and client-server interactions.

issues.title: Remove deprecations from 1.0.0-M6
issues.body:
- [x] Remove deprecations from models, vector store and usage: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2264
- [x] Remove deprecated FunctionCallback references and replace them with ToolCall updates : https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2265

issues.title: Remove deprecated FunctionCalling API references
issues.body: Remove all the deprecations and references of Function Calling APIs and replace them with ToolCalling.
